threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still instinctively check your phone for Tube updates while walking. The locals just glide to the bus stop with resigned confidence.
Caught a nodding bloke clutching a fish and chips box on the 59 last night. He starts chatting about the 'perfect crunch' while the bus hits every pothole.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I spotted a crowd gathering at the corner of Herrfurthstraße, arguing about whether the new vegan café was worth the hype. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you see people sitting in a park, reading books by candlelight at dusk. It's like a mini festival of lumens, so cozy yet so unpretentious.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their skip of the 12 bus when it’s running late—no one who really lives here will waste time playing the waiting game.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy doing yoga on the bus this morning. He was so focused, you'd think he was on the mat at Mission Dolores Park.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia's plazas are just for selfies, but locals actually gather in them to debate everything from politics to the latest vermut spot.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their hesitation at the Strret performers—local kids roll their eyes while the newcomers dig for change.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still look surprised when you see a fox casually walking past while you’re waiting for the bus at 2 AM.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week; the driver sang along to a classic reggae track, turning a packed bus into an impromptu concert. Pure London magic.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The sound of a late-night kebab shop argument outside my window, mixed with the laughter of kids playing in the street. That's when it hit me.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Friedrichshain will you find a bar that serves cocktails in Lego cups. It’s a quirky nod to the childhood nostalgia that locals cherish.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You'll spot a local in the Mission by their ability to name all the taco trucks that vanished, while the newcomers just flock to the same trendy spots.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 47 this morning. The driver announced a detour due to filming. Nothing says San Francisco like a bus pause for a movie shoot!

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all Gràcia bars serve only vermut. Real locals know the best spot's a quiet pintor’s gallery with craft beers no one else finds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a newcomer by their struggle with the escalators at Diagonal metro—always standing still on the left side, while locals breeze past.
